User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the ClinicFlow application.

Income.title: Income
Income.description: Represents an income record for the clinic.

id.description: Unique identifier for the income record.

userId.description: Reference to the User who recorded the income. (Relationship: User 1:N Income)

date.description: The date the income was received.

description.description: A description of the income source.

categoryId.description: Reference to the IncomeCategory that this income belongs to. (Relationship: IncomeCategory 1:N Income)

Expense.title: Expense
Expense.description: Represents an expense record for the clinic.

id.description: Unique identifier for the expense record.

userId.description: Reference to the User who recorded the expense. (Relationship: User 1:N Expense)

date.description: The date the expense was incurred.

categoryId.description: Reference to the ExpenseCategory that this expense belongs to. (Relationship: ExpenseCategory 1:N Expense)

IncomeCategory.title: IncomeCategory
IncomeCategory.description: Represents a category for classifying income sources.

id.description: Unique identifier for the income category.

name.description: The name of the income category.

userId.description: Reference to the User who created the category. (Relationship: User 1:N IncomeCategory)

ExpenseCategory.title: ExpenseCategory
ExpenseCategory.description: Represents a category for classifying expenses.

id.description: Unique identifier for the expense category.

name.description: The name of the expense category.

userId.description: Reference to the User who created the category. (Relationship: User 1:N ExpenseCategory)

Budget.title: Budget
Budget.description: Represents a user's budget for a specific period.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns the budget. (Relationship: User 1:N Budget)

startDate.description: The start date of the budget period.

endDate.description: The end date of the budget period.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. The userId is used to establish ownership and is the key for all subcollections.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores income records for a specific user. Income records are owned by the user identified by userId.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: incomeId
params.description: The unique identifier of the income record.

definition.description: Stores expense records for a specific user. Expense records are owned by the user identified by userId.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: expenseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the expense record.

definition.description: Stores income categories for a specific user. Income categories are owned by the user identified by userId.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: incomeCategoryId
params.description: The unique identifier of the income category.

definition.description: Stores expense categories for a specific user. Expense categories are owned by the user identified by userId.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: expenseCategoryId
params.description: The unique identifier of the expense category.

definition.description: Stores budgets for a specific user. Budgets are owned by the user identified by userId.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: budgetId
params.description: The unique identifier of the budget.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to manage financial data for the ClinicFlow application, focusing on user-specific data with clear ownership and authorization. The structure uses path-based ownership for most entities (Income, Expense, IncomeCategory, ExpenseCategory, Budget), associating each with a specific user. This design ensures that security rules can be easily implemented based on the `request.auth.uid`, allowing users to access only their own data. 

**Authorization Independence:** All data is stored under the `/users/{userId}` path, eliminating the need for `get()` calls in security rules to check parent document attributes. The `userId` is implicitly the owner, ensuring authorization independence. 

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** The path-based structure supports secure list operations. Because each collection (e.g., `/users/{userId}/incomes`) contains only documents owned by that user, listing documents in that collection is inherently secure, as rules only need to verify that `request.auth.uid == userId`.

Each user has their own `Income`, `Expense`, `IncomeCategory`, `ExpenseCategory`, and `Budget` collections, enabling them to track and manage their financial data independently.
